priorFindingsStatus.note: Ground-truthed dex.ts's current catch block directly (src/commands/dex.ts:52-54): `const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err); return fail(msg, opts.json);` — exact match to what REQ-DX-017 quotes as the pre-fix state. Grep-verified across all 18 src/commands/<name>.ts files (excluding shared.ts, which is a helper not a command) that exactly 17 already call `extractErrorMessage(` in their catch (chat, rpc, video, wallet, models, realface, music, speech, markets, image, search, surf, exa, modal, phone, price, defi) and dex.ts is the sole holdout — matches REQ-DX-014's corrected '17 of 18' claim exactly. Confirmed the behavior-preservation claim in REQ-DX-017: dex.ts's only throw site (line 35, `throw new Error(...)`, a bare Error with no .response) and its underlying fetchJson/fetchWithTimeout (src/shell/http.ts) never attach a `.response` property to any thrown error on any path (explicit throw, raw fetch() TypeError, or AbortController timeout) — so extractErrorMessage()'s .response-handling branch (errors.ts:10-27) is unreachable for every error dex.ts can currently produce, making extractErrorMessage(err) === err.message for all of dex's non-object/non-Error and Error-with-no-.response cases, identical to the old inline expression, for every case except REQ-DX-015's new network-failure marker (the intended, additive change). REQ-DX-014/REQ-DX-017 and PROP-DX-006/PROP-DX-014 are internally consistent with each other on this point, and the document's totals (28 REQ-DX-*, 14 PROP-DX-*) were recounted and are numerically correct.

findings.description: PROP-DX-006 step 4's RED-phase requirement for `defi`'s case was corrupted by the same edit that added the `dex` extension (commit d67fda9, spec-review it-2's fix). The original, correct text (commit f132269, it-1's fix) read: 'THIS EXACT TEST, run against today's pre-fix code (before REQ-DX-015's extractErrorMessage() extension exists), SHALL FAIL — because extractErrorMessage() currently collapses the stubbed error to the bare string "fetch failed" with no cause/name information...'. The current text instead reads: 'defi's case, run against code with REQ-DX-015 but WITHOUT REQ-DX-016 applied, SHALL FAIL for the reason originally described (no cause/name info reaches the classifier).' This is self-contradictory and factually wrong: (1) REQ-DX-015 is precisely the fix that makes cause/name info reach the classifier (it appends a marker to the string extractErrorMessage() returns, per REQ-DX-015's own text) — stating the test fails 'for the reason originally described (no cause/name info reaches the classifier)' while simultaneously stating REQ-DX-015 IS applied is a direct contradiction of the premise; (2) REQ-DX-016 (extracting classifyKnownError from formatError() for isModelUnavailable/isServerError/isPaymentError classification) has nothing to do with network_error detection at all — REQ-DX-011's own classification-order clause states network_error (item 6/step (a)) is checked FIRST and independently, with REQ-DX-016's extraction only used for step (b)'s model_unavailable/server_error/payment_error mapping. Gating defi's network_error RED-phase requirement on REQ-DX-016's absence is grounded in nothing in this spec. This makes PROP-DX-006's RED-phase requirement for defi unimplementable as literally written — Phase 2a cannot construct a meaningful 'REQ-DX-015 applied, REQ-DX-016 not applied' intermediate state that fails for the stated reason, because the stated reason is untrue of that state.
findings.suggestedFix: Restore the original, correct semantics from commit f132269: defi's RED-phase requirement is gated on REQ-DX-015 alone (pre-REQ-DX-015 code SHALL FAIL because extractErrorMessage() drops cause/name info; post-REQ-DX-015 code SHALL PASS), not on REQ-DX-016. REQ-DX-016 is unrelated to this PROP's network_error assertion and should not appear in defi's RED-phase clause at all. Keep dex's separate, correctly-reasoned RED-phase requirement (gated on REQ-DX-017, which IS the right dependency for dex) unchanged — only the defi sentence needs the REQ-DX-016 reference removed and the REQ-DX-015 gating language restored to match the original it-1 wording.
